frameworks.name: neural population coding
frameworks.one_line: memory precision modeled as decoded readout of tuned neural populations

frameworks.name: shared-resource model of VWM
frameworks.one_line: memory precision declines continuously as encoded items share limited capacity

frameworks.name: population coding for bounded non-circular variables
frameworks.one_line: circular population code adapted to facial-expression parameter space

core_question: Do shared resource limits and population-coding accounts of visual working memory extend from primary features to high-level objects such as facial expressions?
key_assumptions: facial expression can be reproduced on a continuous bounded scale
population-coding principles generalize across visual hierarchy levels
precision declines smoothly with set size when items share resources
manipulations: memory set size from one to five items
stimulus type: oriented gratings vs facial expressions vs mixed arrays
key_findings: expression-recall precision declined steadily with increasing face load
an adapted population-coding model fit the non-circular expression error distributions well
increasing the number of faces reduced precision of orientation recall
increasing the number of gratings did not impair recall of expressions
interpretations: population-coding principles apply to memory representations at multiple levels of the visual hierarchy
encoding faces automatically engages low-level features, producing an asymmetric cross-feature interference
